key_insight: PINNSur extends physics-informed neural networks to curved surfaces by learning surface normals and projecting 3D differential operators onto the manifold, making PINNs usable for broad classes of orientable surfaces.
engineering_relevance: This offers a mesh-free alternative to FEM for surface PDEs, potentially reducing sensitivity to mesh quality and geometric discretization in geometry processing and scientific computing.
method: Train a neural field to approximate the surface normal vector field.
Define surface differential operators by projecting Euclidean operators from R^3 onto the surface.
Use the framework to solve PDEs directly on curved, orientable manifolds.
evidence_signal: The paper evaluates convergence behavior with a simple empirical convergence test, focusing on whether PINNs can approach the true surface PDE solution as both function and geometry approximations improve.
watch_item: Reliable performance depends on accurately learning normals and geometric approximations, so convergence may be limited by surface representation errors as well as the usual PINN optimization challenges.
paper_title: PINNsur: Physics-Informed Neural Networks for PDEs on Curved Surfaces
